text:
BOTTOM LINE — Chip rebound, defensives got sold.

US equities bounced on AI/chip covering, but the tape was still fragile: QQQ’s -2.00% lag versus IWM’s -1.04% says large-cap growth de-risking was the main event, not a clean risk-on bid. VIX at 22.2 and VXN at 32.7 kept the volatility bid alive while oil up 2.28% and gold down 4.15% pointed to a geopolitics-and-rates mix, not a pure growth scare.

INDICES — VIX 22.2, up. QQQ -2.00%, SPY -1.58%, DIA -1.80%, IWM -1.04%, SOXX -3.67%. STYLE: Small > large by 0.87pp. Biggest cross-asset move: GLD -4.15%.

VOLUME — QQQ vol_ratio 2.3x and SPY vol_ratio 1.9x showed active attention in the selloff; both were price down with volume up, a distribution read. XLI at 1.8x and XLK at 1.4x were also gaining attention, while XLU 0.8x and XLB 0.8x were being ignored.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLP Staples +1.65%, XLE Energy +1.50%, XLU Utilities +0.05%, XLRE Real Estate +0.04%, XLC Comms -0.42%, XLF Financials -0.44%, XLV Healthcare -1.11%, XLY Discretionary -2.05%, XLK Technology -2.29%, XLB Materials -2.30%, XLI Industrials -3.38%. Top industries were FCG +2.46% and XOP +2.40%; bottom were GDX -4.87% and WGMI -5.94%. Money moved into energy and staples, while industrials, materials, and technology were the main sources of money-out. That is defensive rotation with selective commodity and AI-chip rebound underneath: defensive risk-off.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 1.08 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.56 both signaled protection demand, with QQQ notably higher. SPY skew +11.2% and QQQ skew +11.8% showed put premium; VIX at 22.2 versus its 20-day 17.5 confirmed elevated fear.

CROSS-ASSET — USO +2.28% and VIXY +5.81% rose together as geopolitics and hedging demand reinforced each other. GLD -4.15% fell even as risk aversion stayed bid, which is a rates-dominant signal rather than classic flight-to-safety.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 66.1, with Intel +11.0% in Technology, Micron +10.2% in Technology, and Marvell +8.4% in Technology leading the rebound. 20d momentum extremes were XLE +1.18% best, XLB -4.73% worst, FCG strongest basket, and WGMI weakest basket. FNV had a 4.2x vol spike in Precious Metals and fell -1.74%, a distribution read. CCJ -7.08% in Uranium, QQQ -2.00% near elevated vol, and XLI -3.38% deserve attention tomorrow for rotation follow-through and continued de-risking.
